question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user craft stream files with symlink-like filenames that when isWriteFiles() calls Utility.archiveFile() cause the importer to write outside the intended data directory, potentially filling a critical filesystem partition and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RECORD StreamType being enabled=true by default to ensure the record downloader is always active, and by continuously injecting files that pass signature verification but fail hash chain validation cause the importer to loop indefinitely retrying, exhausting CPU in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of isPersistBytes()=true and the absence of a maximum blob size constraint in the database schema to insert a single 50MiB blob that causes PostgreSQL TOAST table fragmentation, degrading query performance across the entire mirror node database and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonDownloaderProperties sources list by staging files that cause all configured sources to fail simultaneously, triggering the backoff=60s retry loop across all sources and starving the downloader thread pool of available workers, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch of batchSize=25 signature files per node, each at maximum size, such that when isWriteSignatures() is true the importer writes 25 * N_nodes * maxSigSize bytes to disk per batch cycle, rapidly exhausting disk space and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getFrequency() returns a Duration with no minimum enforcement at the DownloaderProperties interface level, and if a misconfiguration sets frequency to 1ms, cause the importer to spin at 1000 iterations/second exhausting CPU and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a timestamp just 1 nanosecond after the configured endDate, causing setEnabled(false) to be called after processing the very first file, permanently halting all subsequent record ingestion and causing a non-network DoS for the process lifetime?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setPersistBytes() setter being publicly accessible on the interface to cause a scenario where bytes are persisted for every file in a high-frequency polling cycle, causing the database transaction log to grow unboundedly and exhaust disk space in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonDownloaderProperties maxConcurrency=1000 HTTP connections by staging files that cause 1000 simultaneous in-flight downloads each holding a 50MiB buffer, exhausting 50GiB of JVM heap and causing an OutOfMemoryError non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user craft stream files with timestamps that produce filenames colliding with existing archived files, causing isWriteFiles() to trigger repeated file overwrite operations that corrupt the archive and cause the importer to re-download and re-process files indefinitely in a non-network DoS loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BALANCE StreamType by injecting crafted balance files that contain an extremely large number of account entries, causing the balance parser to allocate a batchSize=200000 entry buffer that exhausts JVM heap and causes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between isPersistBytes()=true and the parser's batch.queueCapacity=10 to fill the in-memory queue with 10 * 50MiB = 500MiB of raw byte blobs, causing JVM heap exhaustion and a non-network DoS before any data is flushed to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonDownloaderProperties downloadRatio=1 (download from all nodes) by staging crafted files on all nodes simultaneously, causing the importer to download N_nodes * batchSize * maxSize bytes per cycle and exhaust both network bandwidth and memory in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user craft signature files with filenames containing null bytes or special characters that when isWriteSignatures() calls archiveFile() cause the JVM to throw uncaught exceptions that crash the downloader thread, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the balance downloader's getFrequency()=30s to stage a new 50MiB balance file every 30 seconds, and when isPersistBytes()=true cause a sustained 50MiB/30s = ~1.7MiB/s database write rate that exhausts storage over time in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) is called from within the download processing loop without any re-enable mechanism, meaning a single crafted file with a future timestamp permanently disables the downloader for the JVM process lifetime, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that is valid enough to pass signature verification but contains a maximally large bytes payload, and when isPersistBytes()=true causes a single database INSERT of 50MiB that exceeds PostgreSQL's max_wal_size, causing WAL overflow and a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonDownloaderProperties batchSize=25 by staging exactly 25 files per node that all fail hash chain verification, causing the importer to download and attempt to verify 25 * N_nodes files per cycle without making progress, wasting CPU and I/O in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user craft stream files that when archived via isWriteFiles() produce an extremely large number of small files in the data directory, causing the filesystem's directory entry table to overflow and making ls/stat operations O(n), degrading importer performance in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RECORD StreamType's enabled=true default and 500ms frequency to inject a continuous stream of files that each trigger a full database transaction, causing PostgreSQL autovacuum to run continuously and exhaust I/O capacity in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between isPersistBytes()=true and the record parser's batch.maxItems=60000 to cause a single batch to hold 60000 items each with a 50MiB byte blob reference, exhausting JVM heap and causing a non-network DoS before the batch is flushed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonDownloaderProperties sources list being mutable (ArrayList) to cause a scenario where source failover logic iterates over a growing list of failed sources, consuming increasing CPU per download cycle and causing a non-network DoS through algorithmic complexity?
